notable_excerpts: The EV revenues in Quarter 2 were 18% of domestic revenues.
We have a very vigorous R&D effort going on for development of electric motorcycles.
We expect E-auto performance to get unlocked due to the removal of supply constraints.
The mobility needs of middle India are expanding furiously and we expect the three-wheeler category to continue to grow.
Supply chain constraints resulting in an almost 50% shortfall to our plan.
Early feedback from customers is very positive and it allows us to formulate our go-to-market plan.
BACL continues to enjoy the highest level of creditworthiness with AAA ratings.
We have shifted to the more secure LRE-based components as well as alternate geographic sources.
The market share erosion which we experienced over the last few quarters had got arrested by the end of Quarter 2.

chain_of_thought: The earnings call highlighted Bajaj Auto's focus on expanding its electric vehicle portfolio and addressing supply chain constraints, which are key environmental themes. Social aspects were touched upon with community impact and supply chain issues. Governance was discussed in terms of risk management and maintaining market share. The disclosures were moderately detailed and positive, leading to a balanced ESG score.
strengths: Strong focus on electric vehicle development
red_flags: Significant supply chain disruptions affecting EV segment
